I read the prompt and prior log as a proposal record, not as proof. The prompt fixes the tame-format submission with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and exponents modulo M=85667662080. The late prior log leaves the dynamic tame-inertia word as the only serious unrefuted candidate, but Turn 74 reopened one universal square coordinate œµb after noticing that [‚àí1] is the torsion line, not the free b-line.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with œÄ=œÄ2‚ààZ and Œ∑=2œÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,where œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑‚â°50932630783(modM).I keep the late-turn stable algebra in equation form. For the sign-cover norm coefficient,Da=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j satisfies (1‚àítŒ±a)Da=1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ=1‚àít,soDa=(1‚àít)(1‚àítŒ±a)‚àí1.For the trivial tame-inertia block, the safe elimination polynomial is defined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚àís,s‚â°œÄ(modf),up to the harmless T‚ÜîT‚àí1 convention. This avoids the recurring illegal ‚Äúdivision by 1+T‚Äù phrasing in F2 [T]/(Tf‚àí1).

New Turn 75 point: the proposed b‚à®=[5] normalization is false in the unramified quadratic test

Turn 74‚Äôs proposed next step was to prove uniformly that the free b-line is the rational order-four unit class [5]. That cannot be right as stated. The unramified quadratic case already gives a counterexample to that normalization, while still matching the candidate‚Äôs mod-four Frobenius action.LetL=Q2 (œâ),œâ2+œâ+1=0,and let F be arithmetic Frobenius, so F(œâ)=œâ2=‚àí1‚àíœâ. Use the same arithmetic square-class representatives already isolated in the prior unramified-quadratic check:m=‚àí1,u=1+2œâ,v=1+2(1+œâ)=3+2œâ.In the Turn 16/17 notation, the invariant b-class is represented by B=umv=‚àí(1+2œâ)(3+2œâ)=1‚àí4œâ.Compare this with the rational unit 5=1+4. I claim B is not equal to 5 in L√ó/L√ó2.

Indeed,

B/5‚â°1+4œâ2 (mod8OL).

For an unramified dyadic field, any square in 1+4OL is congruent modulo 8 to1+4(rÀâ+rÀâ2)for somerÀâ‚ààF4. The Artin‚ÄìSchreier image {rÀâ+rÀâ2:rÀâ‚ààF4} is {0,1}. Since œâ2‚àà/{0,1}, the unit 1+4œâ2 is not a square. ThereforeBÓÄÝ=5in¬ÝL√ó/L√ó2.

So the plan ‚Äúprove b‚à®=[5] uniformly‚Äù is not merely unproved; it is false under the natural unramified-quadratic arithmetic normalization already used in the prior log.

But the candidate still passes the corrected œµb check in this case

The important distinction is that œµb=0 does not require b=[5]. It requires Frobenius not to add a 2b term in L√ó/L√ó4.

Compute Frobenius on B=1‚àí4œâ:

F(B)=1‚àí4œâ2=5+4œâ.ThenBF(B)=1‚àí4œâ5+4œâ.Nowv2=(3+2œâ)2=5+8œâ.

A direct calculation givesBF(B)‚ãÖv‚àí2=1+16‚ãÖ(an¬Ýelement¬Ýof¬ÝOL).

Since the 2-adic logarithm identifies 1+4OL with 4OL, every element of 1+16OL is a fourth power. Therefore

F(B)‚â°Bv2 (modL√ó4).This is exactly the candidate-side mod-four action in the unramified quadratic kernel. From the exact candidate automorphism recorded in the prior log,Œ±(b)=a1a0cba1‚àí1a0c‚àí1,so after abelianization over Z2/4,Œ±(b)=b+2a0.

Under the arithmetic identification a0 ‚Üîv, this agrees with

F(B)=Bv2 (modL√ó4).

Thus the corrected conclusion is:œµb=0 in the unramified quadratic test, but not because b is the rational class [5]. Instead, Frobenius moves b by twice a regular-unit class.

This is a useful correction to both Turn 73 and Turn 74:

Turn 73 was wrong to kill œµb using [‚àí1].

Turn 74 was right to reopen œµb, but its proposed b‚à®=[5] normalization is not the right way to close it.The right test is the direct mod-four arithmetic action on the b-class, allowing regular-square correction terms.For odd-order tame quotients, b=[5]-type rational normalization may still be recoverable because odd-order cohomology has no extra invariant classes. But for even f, inflation-restriction allows invariant squareclasses not coming from Q2√ó, and the unramified quadratic B=1‚àí4œâ is the first example.

So the first-IA obstruction should still be treated as(œµb,R(T))‚ààF2‚äïF2[Cd],but œµb should be computed by the mod-four quotientBarithmetic¬ÝFrobenius(B)projected onto the b-square direction, not by trying to identify b with [5].

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I found that Turn 74‚Äôs proposed normalization b‚à®=[5] is false already for L/Q2 unramified quadratic: the candidate‚Äôs b-class is represented by B=1‚àí4œâ, and B/5 is not a square. However, the corrected œµb test still passes in this case, because F(B)‚â°Bv2 (modL√ó4), matching the candidate action Œ±(b)=b+2a0 on abelianization mod 4.

GAP: The presentation is still not proved. The first-IA arithmetic-minus-candidate obstruction remains (œµb,R(T)), but œµb must be computed directly from mod-four Kummer action, not killed by a rational [5] normalization. The mixed anti-cyclotomic residuals Rc (T) still have not been computed.

NEXT: Generalize the unramified quadratic computation: construct the candidate b-class BL for finite tame L/Q2, compute Œ≥(BL)/BL in L√ó/L√ó4 for tame Frobenius Œ≥, and prove its projection to the b-square direction is zero. Then compute the primitive mixed residuals using Œîc(T)Rc (T) for c=3,5,9,11.[BREAKTHROUGH]
